summary.intent: Add archive/hide functionality for sessions on the main screen, a sync button to refresh session state from `entire status`, and fix the arrow button to open sessions in a new in-app tab instead of using AppleScript.
summary.outcome: All three features were implemented: archive/unarchive buttons per session row, an archived sessions toggle, a sync endpoint, and a new LauncherScreen that opens resumed sessions as in-app tabs via WebSocket terminal.

repo: The gossamer database uses FTS5 virtual tables (SQLite full-text search), which are incompatible with `prisma db push` — schema migrations must be done via raw SQL executed on startup instead.
Session indexing runs via a shadow branch poller (`runAllShadows`) every 5 seconds; the sync endpoint triggers this immediately rather than waiting for the next poll.
The app uses a tab system with typed tab kinds (e.g. 'launcher'); new tab types require wiring in both TabsContext.tsx (creation) and Shell.tsx (rendering).
SessionRow was intentionally kept decoupled from TabsContext — resume behavior is passed in as an `onResume` callback from ActiveSessions rather than imported directly.
The LauncherScreen connects via WebSocket and uses a 300ms delay before typing the command to allow the shell to initialize.

code.finding: ArchivedSession table is created via raw SQL on server startup because prisma db push fails with FTS5 virtual tables present in the schema. The GET /api/sessions endpoint filters archived sessions by default and accepts ?archived=1 to show only archived ones.
ArchivedSession model was added to the Prisma schema for type generation, but actual table creation bypasses prisma db push due to FTS5 incompatibility.
New screen that renders a full-height xterm terminal connecting via WebSocket, typing a provided command after a 300ms initialization delay.
Added openLauncherTab(cwd, command, title) which creates a uniquely-keyed launcher tab each call, enabling multiple simultaneous resume sessions.

workflow: When Prisma schema changes conflict with custom SQLite features (like FTS5), bypass prisma db push and use raw SQL migrations executed at application startup.
Building both the server (tsc) and Electron app separately helps catch TypeScript errors in each layer independently.

friction: prisma db push failed due to FTS5 virtual tables in the existing schema, requiring a workaround of creating the ArchivedSession table via direct SQLite raw SQL.
Multiple iterative edits were needed to SessionRow and ActiveSessions to correctly wire the onResume callback pattern, suggesting the component interfaces weren't immediately obvious.
The original arrow button used AppleScript for session resumption rather than the in-app tab system, indicating the tab system was added after the initial session resume flow was built.
open_items: The ArchivedSession table creation bypasses Prisma migrations entirely — future schema changes will need to manually handle this table outside of the normal migration workflow.
The LauncherScreen uses a hardcoded 300ms delay for shell initialization which may be unreliable on slow systems.
No unarchive flow is surfaced from the main screen by default — users must toggle the 'Archived' switch to find and restore hidden sessions.
